output.title: Landlord Withholding Security Deposit? Recover Your Money in 3 Steps
output.meta_description: Your landlord is withholding your security deposit illegally? Learn your tenant rights, what deductions are legal, and how to demand your money back in days.
content_outline: The Breaking Point: When Your Landlord Withholds Your Security Deposit
Is My Landlord Required to Return My Deposit? State Deadlines Explained
What Can a Landlord Deduct (and What Is Illegal)?
“My Landlord Won’t Give Me My Deposit Back”—Your Immediate Action Plan
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
Fast-Track Your Recovery: Send Your Demand Letter Today
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days
Normal wear and tear cannot be deducted; only documented damage beyond reasonable use is legal
Tenants who send a formal demand letter recover deposits up to 3x faster than those who only call or email
A security deposit dispute letter citing state law signals you know your rights and are prepared to escalate
DepositBack automates state-specific demand letters so you can demand your deposit back in minutes, not weeks
output.cta: Don’t let your landlord keep what’s yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter now and start your recovery today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters dealing with landlords withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting security deposits who need immediate legal leverage to recover their money.
